title: ADPA Template Lifecycle, Versioning, and AI Feedback Loop Enhancement
objectives: Transform ADPA templates into dynamic, continuously improving intelligence assets.
Establish explicit, auditable lifecycle states and robust versioning for templates and documents.
Enable AI-driven quality audits, optimization, and improvement loops.
Maintain human-in-the-loop governance for high-risk changes, with controlled automation for low-risk updates.
Ensure traceability, rollback capability, and comprehensive observability for all template changes.

stakeholders.name: Project Sponsor
responsibilities: Approve major project decisions and deliverables.

stakeholders.name: Project Manager
responsibilities: Oversee project planning, execution, and delivery.
Coordinate stakeholders and manage risks, timeline, and budget.

stakeholders.name: ADPA Engineering Team
responsibilities: Design and implement lifecycle and versioning framework.
Integrate AI feedback loops and automation features.
Ensure backend and service architecture compatibility.

stakeholders.name: AI/ML Team
responsibilities: Develop and maintain AI-driven quality audit and optimization services.
Define and tune automation thresholds and policies.

stakeholders.name: Quality Assurance
responsibilities: Validate template changes for quality and compliance.

stakeholders.name: Human Reviewers
responsibilities: Review and approve high-risk or structural template changes.

stakeholders.name: Operations Team
responsibilities: Manage rollout, rollback, and regeneration operations.

requirements: Implement a template lifecycle management system with explicit states and auditable transitions.
Develop robust versioning and traceability for templates and documents.
Integrate AI-driven feedback loops for continuous quality audits and optimization.
Support human-in-the-loop controls for high-risk changes and confidence-based automation for low-risk updates.
Enable rollback and regeneration operations with preserved document-template lineage.
Provide comprehensive observability and audit logging.
Ensure compliance with all relevant standards and policies.
deliverables: Template lifecycle management framework with explicit states and transitions.
Versioning system with traceable lineage for templates and documents.
Integrated AI feedback loop for quality audits and optimization.
Human-in-the-loop governance workflows and automation policies.
Rollback and regeneration tools with lineage preservation.
Documentation for processes, policies, and user training.
success_criteria: 100% of template changes are tracked with explicit lifecycle states and version history.
All production template changes pass through gated validation and approval.
AI-driven quality audits are operational and provide actionable recommendations.
Human-in-the-loop governance is enforced for all high-risk changes.
Rollback and regeneration operations preserve document-template lineage without data loss.
Audit and observability data is available and queryable for compliance and operational review.
Reduction in template-related errors and compliance issues by at least 30% within 6 months of deployment.
assumptions: ADPA backend and service architecture supports integration of new lifecycle and versioning models.
AI services can provide reliable quality audits and optimization recommendations.
Human reviewers are available for approval of high-risk or structural changes.
Automation thresholds and policies can be defined and tuned based on template class and risk.
Audit, lineage, and observability data can be persisted and queried efficiently.
constraints: All production template changes must pass through gated validation and approval.
Automation is limited to high-confidence, low-risk changes with rollback paths.
Regeneration and rollback operations must preserve document-template lineage.
Bulk operations require concurrency and throttling controls.
Compliance and standards changes require manual approval.

risks.description: Integration complexity with existing ADPA backend and service architecture.
risks.mitigation: Conduct a technical feasibility assessment and pilot integration before full rollout.

risks.description: Reliability and accuracy of AI-driven quality audits and recommendations.
risks.mitigation: Implement pilot testing, continuous monitoring, and regular model retraining.

risks.description: Availability of human reviewers for high-risk or structural changes.
risks.mitigation: Define clear reviewer roles, allocate resources, and consider reviewer rotation.

risks.description: Potential delays due to manual approvals for compliance and standards changes.
risks.mitigation: Streamline approval workflows and set SLAs for review times.

risks.description: Operational risks related to automation thresholds and rollback mechanisms.
risks.mitigation: Establish conservative automation thresholds and robust rollback procedures with monitoring.

breakdown: [PLACEHOLDER] Cost estimate - To be determined during project planning phase

resources: Infrastructure for audit, observability, and rollback operations
